What Motionfly generates [...] Product Launch Videos

# AI Product Launch Video Generator

Create stunning product launch videos that captivate your audience, with cinematic 3D mockups, professional animations, and AI voiceover. All from a single text prompt.

An AI product launch video generator turns a written description of your product into a finished launch video, with 3D device mockups, animated text, voiceover, and background music, in under a minute. Motionfly is a prompt-to-video tool built specifically for SaaS, mobile apps, and physical products that need a polished launch announcement without hiring a motion designer or learning After Effects.

Get Started for Free

## Product launches deserve great videos. Most teams can't afford them. [...] ### Background Music & Sound FX

Curated background music and effect sounds automatically synced to your video's pacing for a polished result.

### Multiple Video Formats

Export in 16:9 landscape, 9:16 vertical (Stories/Reels), or 1:1 square, optimized for every platform.

### Instant Iteration

Don't like the result? Tweak your prompt and regenerate. No waiting days for revision rounds.

## How it works

1

### Describe your product

Write a prompt describing your product, its key features, and your target audience. Be as specific or as brief as you want, Motionfly adapts to your input.

Motionfly's AI creates a complete launch video with 3D mockups, animated text, logo reveals, voiceover, background music, and sound effects, all in under a minute.

## From design experiments to Replit Animation

Replit Animation started as a design experiment. When the team launched Replit Design on top of Gemini, Samuel wondered if the same setup that generated sites and decks could also drive animation.

results.title: What tools do you use for product launch videos?
results.content: - For clean launch videos, I’d pair After Effects (or Cavalry) for motion with DaVinci Resolve for editing, plus a tight Figma-to-AE handoff. AEUX or Overlord to bring vectors/UI in cleanly; Animation Composer and Flow for easy easing presets; Rotato or Spline for device shots; CleanShot X for sharp screen captures; CapCut for fast captions; Descript for script/VO; Runway for quick background cleanup and b-roll; Topaz Video AI to upscale screen grabs. Keep it 20–45 seconds, open with the payoff in 2–3 seconds, show only 2–3 core flows, big on-screen text per beat, tight zooms, fake cursor with motion blur, and export 16:9, 1:1, and 9:16 with burned captions. I’ve used Descript and Runway for speed, and Pulse for Reddit helps during launch week to track threads and draft quick replies [...] - Most of those slick launch videos aren't using some secret AI tool, they're just well-executed After Effects work combined with good screen recordings. The motion graphics part is usually templates from VideoHive or custom animations built in After Effects. For the actual screen recording beyond Screen Studio, CleanShot X is solid for Mac users. Loom works for basic stuff but the quality isn't great for polished launch videos. Our clients who do serious product videos usually combine Screen Studio for the recordings with After Effects for the motion graphics and transitions. Figma to After Effects workflows are huge right now. Design your interface animations in Figma, export to After Effects, then composite with your screen recordings. Way cleaner than trying to animate everything from [...] - CANVID . It's an alternative to Screen Studio that works on macOS & Windows.

results.title: Top 10 Product Launch Video Agencies To Choose in 2026
results.content:
What problems do you see? How are people frustrated with these problems, looked for solutions but couldn’t get them solved, and how does your product present an unprecedented solution?

As we discussed, you have the information on all the features, benefits, and solutions your product brings to your potential customers.

You should be able to create a script using all this important information. There should be a strong message for the audience. The script's flow should complement the video.

The product launch video is an announcement video, but it's also easy to understand for someone with zero knowledge.

Your goal with the script is to find a balance that caters to experts in the field and to someone who’s completely unfamiliar with your industry.   
‍ [...] 1. Problem in the Hook: Your product launch video will immediately jump to the viewers' pain points or challenges.
2. Your product as a solution: Quickly present your product as a solution to the problem.
3. Prove your point: You established your product as a solution; show it in action, demonstrating how it solves the problem.
4. Emotional Connection/Anticipation: Use high-quality visuals, music, and pacing to create excitement and desire.
5. Single, Clear Call-to-Action (CTA): Tell the viewer exactly what to do next (e.g., "Pre-order Now" or "Sign Up").

## What Should You Consider While Looking for a Product Launch Video Agency?

Explainer Videos [...] ## 7. Customer Fields

Customer Fields is a Shopify app that helps eCommerce operators add features to their online store. The video uses shapes to communicate the frustrations of a restrictive platform like Shopify. If a user watches the video they will instantly relate to how difficult it is customizing their Shopify store. By the end of the product launch animated video, the shapes align, thanks to Customer Fields, and harmony is restored in the Shopify universe.

## 8. GoPro HERO 10 Black

GoPro is uniquely positioned to take advantage of user-generated content in its marketing. When possible, showing the product in action makes for incredible product reveal videos. [...] ## 19. Presenting Sendcloud

2D animated explainers have become the standard for SaaS product launches. Producing over 100 videos per year, we sometimes hit a creative crisis. But this video opened my eyes.

Sendcloud’s requirement was clear: show the problem and the solution. Most videos of this type focus on one general problem, which is fine. But here, the creative team excelled with the script and voiceover. By listing problems with single words and accelerating the narrator's tempo, you feel personally connected, even if the issues aren't directly relevant to you.

Combined with a remarkable style and dynamic animation, this video deserves a spot in the hall of fame for short animated ads.
